tasks.title: Puppy training session
tasks.reason: Puppies have a critical socialization window; short, consistent training sessions build obedience and reduce future behavioral issues

tasks.title: Morning meal
tasks.reason: Puppies need 3-4 small meals per day to support rapid growth and maintain stable blood sugar

tasks.title: Midday meal
tasks.reason: Frequent feeding prevents hypoglycemia in young puppies whose metabolism is fast

tasks.title: Potty break and short walk
tasks.reason: Puppies need frequent bathroom breaks every 2-3 hours; outdoor exposure also builds confidence and sensory experience

tasks.title: Socialization play
tasks.reason: Play with toys and gentle handling during the 3-12 week socialization window reduces fear responses in adulthood

tasks.title: Coat and paw check
tasks.reason: Introducing grooming early desensitizes the puppy to handling; also checks for fleas, skin irritation, or nail overgrowth

tasks.title: Morning walk
tasks.reason: Adult dogs need 30-60 min of exercise daily to maintain healthy weight, cardiovascular fitness, and mental well-being

tasks.title: Morning meal
tasks.reason: Consistent feeding times regulate digestion and help prevent bloat, especially in larger breeds

tasks.title: Evening walk
tasks.reason: A second daily walk provides mental stimulation through scent exploration and prevents boredom-related destructive behavior

tasks.title: Interactive play session
tasks.reason: Fetch, tug, and puzzle toys satisfy natural prey drive and strengthen the human-dog bond

tasks.title: Brushing and grooming
tasks.reason: Regular brushing reduces shedding, prevents matting, and allows early detection of lumps or skin changes

tasks.title: Teeth brushing
tasks.reason: Dental disease affects 80% of dogs over age 3; daily brushing is the most effective prevention

tasks.title: Gentle walk
tasks.reason: Low-impact daily movement maintains joint mobility and muscle mass in senior dogs; shorter duration prevents overexertion

tasks.title: Medication administration
tasks.reason: Senior dogs often require joint supplements, heart medication, or pain management; consistent timing ensures therapeutic efficacy

tasks.title: Morning meal
tasks.reason: Senior dogs benefit from smaller, more digestible meals; senior-formula food supports kidney function and joint health

tasks.title: Joint massage and gentle stretching
tasks.reason: Gentle massage improves circulation and helps manage arthritis pain; also a bonding activity that reduces stress

tasks.title: Cognitive enrichment
tasks.reason: Mental stimulation through sniff games and gentle puzzles helps slow cognitive decline in senior dogs

tasks.title: Health check and comfort monitoring
tasks.reason: Weekly checks for lumps, weight changes, mobility shifts, and appetite changes enable early detection of age-related conditions

tasks.title: Kitten meal
tasks.reason: Kittens need calorie-dense kitten formula 3-4 times daily to support rapid growth; free feeding can cause obesity later

tasks.title: Interactive wand play
tasks.reason: Play mimics hunting behavior, providing essential physical and mental stimulation; builds agility and reduces destructive behavior

tasks.title: Litter box cleaning
tasks.reason: Kittens are fastidious; a dirty box leads to avoidance and litter box aversion habits that persist into adulthood

tasks.title: Handling and socialization
tasks.reason: Gentle daily handling during the 2-9 week socialization period produces cats that are comfortable with people and vet visits

tasks.title: Grooming introduction
tasks.reason: Introducing brushing while young builds tolerance; also removes loose hair that can become hairballs

tasks.title: Morning meal
tasks.reason: Consistent feeding times support digestive rhythm; portion control prevents the feline obesity epidemic

tasks.title: Interactive play session
tasks.reason: Cats need 15-20 min of active play daily to maintain healthy weight, reduce stress, and prevent boredom-related aggression

tasks.title: Litter box cleaning
tasks.reason: A clean box prevents inappropriate elimination; the general rule is one box per cat plus one extra

tasks.title: Brushing
tasks.reason: Regular brushing reduces hairballs by up to 85%, and helps detect skin issues or unusual lumps early

tasks.title: Weight and body check
tasks.reason: Cats mask illness; a weekly hands-on check can catch unexplained weight loss, lumps, or coat changes that signal health problems

tasks.title: Senior diet meal
tasks.reason: Senior cats benefit from high-protein, low-phosphorus diets to support kidney function; wet food helps hydration for cats prone to kidney disease

tasks.title: Medication administration
tasks.reason: Many senior cats require daily medication for hyperthyroidism, kidney disease, or arthritis; missed doses impact disease management

tasks.title: Litter box cleaning
tasks.reason: Senior cats with arthritis may avoid a box that requires jumping; low-entry boxes and daily cleaning encourage proper use

tasks.title: Gentle brushing
tasks.reason: Senior cats groom themselves less effectively; brushing prevents matting and allows skin inspection for age-related changes

tasks.title: Comfort and warmth check
tasks.reason: Senior cats have reduced ability to thermoregulate; ensuring access to warm resting spots prevents hypothermia-related health decline

tasks.title: Weekly health monitoring
tasks.reason: Senior cats should be assessed weekly for weight loss, vomiting, changes in thirst or urination — early signals of kidney disease or hyperthyroidism

tasks.title: Feeding
tasks.reason: Regular, species-appropriate feeding maintains energy levels and prevents nutritional deficiencies

tasks.title: Exercise or activity time
tasks.reason: Physical activity prevents obesity and boredom-related behavioral issues regardless of species

tasks.title: Habitat cleaning
tasks.reason: A clean living environment prevents pathogen buildup and reduces stress for the animal

tasks.title: Health check
tasks.reason: Regular observation of behavior, appetite, and physical condition allows early detection of illness
